channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Quality of Life & PROs
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Quality of Life & PROs

item.title: Genetic Etiologies and Risk Factors for Regressive Autism and Childhood Disintegrative Disorder: A Scoping Review.
item.description: Journal of child neurology - Review - Autism spectrum disorder (autism) is diagnosed by persistent deficits in communication and social interaction, along with restricted, repetitive behaviors or interests. About a third of children wi...

item.title: Acute Kidney Injury Associated with Boswellia serrata for Radiation Necrosis.
item.description: Practical radiation oncology - Boswellia serrata is an herbal natural health product derived from the Boswellia serrata tree. Although not approved by regulatory agencies, it possesses anti-inflammatory properties and is used fo...

item.title: The implementation of mindfulness-based cognitive therapy for patients with inflammatory bowel disease: a qualitative study.
item.description: BMC psychology - Our recent trial studying mindfulness-based cognitive therapy (MBCT) for Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD) patients demonstrated reductions in psychological distress and improvements in well-being. ...

item.title: Patient Preferences for Virtual Versus In-Person Care in Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Cross-Sectional Study.
item.description: Telemedicine journal and e-health : the official journal of the American Telemedicine Association - Telemedicine adoption in gastroenterology has accelerated rapidly in recent years. Virtual interventions offer disease activity control comparable to standard in-person care. However, factors deter...

item.title: Digital self-management programme for pain, fatigue and faecal incontinence in inflammatory bowel disease: cost-effectiveness analysis of the IBD-BOOST randomised controlled trial.
item.description: BMJ open gastroenterology - RCT - People living with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) frequently experience abdominal pain, fatigue and faecal incontinence that persist despite optimal medical treatment. This study aimed to assess ...

item.title: Caregiver burden in owners of dogs with chronic enteropathies-a survey-based assessment.
item.description: Journal of veterinary internal medicine - Chronic enteropathy (CE) in dogs requires lifelong management and may cause caregiver burden (CB), potentially affecting owner well-being and, indirectly, veterinarians. We aimed to determine wheth...

item.title: Engineering immune cell therapies for inflammatory bowel disease: from stem cells to CAR T cells.
item.description: Nature reviews. Gastroenterology & hepatology - Review - Despite the development of novel small molecules and antibody-based therapies targeting pro-inflammatory immune pathways, the clinical management of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) remains challen...

item.title: Cajaninstilbene acid in macrophage immunometabolism and angiogenesis: Mechanisms and therapeutic potential.
item.description: Pharmacological research - Review - Macrophage immunometabolism is an important regulator of inflammatory resolution and angiogenic responses. Aberrant macrophage polarization, accompanied by metabolic disturbances such as enhanced g...
